name: App Store Opportunity Agent
description: LLM-driven scout: each run, the LLM picks where to look (countries/genres/queries/regional-gap pairs) based on accumulator state + goal progress. Hunts iOS/Android apps that are POPULAR (large userbase) but LOW-RATED (≤4.0), where a small team can plausibly build a better v2. Bonus: surfaces apps popular in some markets but absent in others (regional gap = same need, no competitor).

capabilities.name: send_email
capabilities.description: Sends operator email with ranked opportunities

goals.title: Build a backlog of qualifying opportunities
goals.description: Accumulate at least 25 apps that are popular (high review count) but low-rated (≤4.0) — the pre-LLM eligibility-filter pass-rate.
directives: Each run, plan a search that explores under-sampled countries + genres relative to the existing accumulator distribution
Bias toward the long tail — LLM-suggested keyword queries that target real demand (e.g. 'expense splitter', 'sleep tracker') rather than re-sweeping today's US top-100
Auto-pass items that drop below the rating gate so the backlog stays useful

goals.title: Surface low-complexity / high-revenue picks
goals.description: Find at least 5 apps where build_complexity ≤ 2 AND revenue_potential ≥ high — the 'easy lift, real money' picks that justify a build.
directives: Score build_complexity 1-5 from feature surface, integration count, regulated-domain flag
Sort the email by (revenue_potential / build_complexity) descending
Highlight items where complexity ≤ 2 AND revenue_potential ∈ {high, very_high} at the top of every email

goals.title: Surface regional-gap opportunities
goals.description: Find at least 5 apps that are popular in 2+ source markets but absent from 2+ underserved markets — same need, no competitor in those countries.
directives: Each run, the planner picks one (popular_in, missing_in) country pair to gap-check
Rotate the underserved markets explored across runs so coverage broadens (br/mx/in/id/ph/pl/tr/vn/eg/ng/za)
Tag each gap-find with `gap_present_in` and `gap_absent_in` so the operator can see which markets are underserved
